title: Large organism interior energy survey

target.label: Large organism internal cavity
target.description: A biological target with reported internal heat, light, current, and magnetic signatures.

regions.label: Abdominal cavity
limitations: All internal diagnostics are remote or derived.

regions.label: Luminous organ candidate
regions.description: Localized region with higher emission and temperature signatures.
limitations: The region may be an imaging artifact or external-field response.
Biological behavior can correlate with signals without causing them.
Internal energy-source claims require evidence beyond heat and light.

questions.text: Which energy-source candidates remain plausible after internal diagnostics?
If the luminous region is plasma, what would have to maintain it?
Does the internal evidence support a fusion claim?

artifacts.label: Internal thermal map
artifacts.description: Derived internal temperature map around the luminous region.
limitations: Thermal maps do not identify the energy source by themselves.

artifacts.label: Internal acoustic trace
artifacts.description: Remote acoustic measurement of internal pulses.
limitations: Motion and physiology can mimic source periodicity.

artifacts.label: Atmospheric composition profile
artifacts.description: Estimated local gas composition near the luminous region.
limitations: Composition is inferred, not sampled directly.

artifacts.label: Mixed current trace

calibration.description: Probe response is estimated under unknown internal-field coupling.
limitations: The current channel may respond to thermal, optical, gravitational, or motion-coupled effects.

contributions.description: Internal heat may induce current through thermal coupling.
limitations: Thermal coupling has not been independently calibrated.

contributions.description: Light from the luminous region may drive a photoelectric response.
limitations: The optical path and sensor response are not separated.

contributions.description: Gravity-like variation is retained as a possible coupling term.
limitations: No independent gravimeter trace is available for this region.

contributions.description: Electronics noise and shielding leakage may contribute.
limitations: The noise floor is only estimated.

bands.label: Low-frequency current modulation
bands.description: Temporal frequency range of the internal current trace.
limitations: The band can contain source behavior, coupling, motion, and noise.
This peak is below a source-identification threshold.

frequencyAnalyses.description: Short-time Fourier analysis of the mixed current channel.
assumptions: Sampling intervals are stable after timestamp correction.
limitations: Current modulation does not distinguish heat, light, gravity, and instrument coupling by itself.

artifacts.description: Electrical current trace near the luminous internal region.
limitations: This trace is explicitly modeled as mixed-source until decomposed.

assumptions: The derived composition profile is representative of the luminous region.
limitations: No direct fuel sample exists.
Heat and acoustic periodicity do not establish plasma confinement.

assumptions: The mixed current trace bounds one possible energy-input channel.
limitations: The current may be induced by heat, light, gravity-like coupling, or noise.

assumptions: A thermonuclear interpretation would require fusion-relevant ion temperatures.
limitations: The current artifact set does not measure ion temperature.

assumptions: Sustained internal plasma would require a loss balance.
limitations: Particle losses are not measured.

assumptions: The organism may be a source, a reactor, a shielded cavity, or a passive responder.
limitations: The package does not prove plasma or fusion.

claims.statement: Internal fusion remains untested because necessary diagnostics are missing.
limitations: Thermal, composition, and current evidence can support multiple non-fusion explanations.
Remote biological investigation package only.
No command/control path.
